1_thailand_applicability.rationale: Multiple Thai sources (KU central-Thailand cherry tomato manual, KU pot-cultivation journal article, RSPG botanical database) confirm tomato is widely cultivated across Thailand both commercially and in home gardens; FAO temperature ranges anchor the cool-season recommendation.

2_climate.rationale: FAO Land & Water provides standard temperature, humidity, water and growing-period parameters; the Biology 2022 review corroborates temperature/light/salinity effects; KU central-Thailand source confirms Thai field reality.

3_soil.rationale: FAO gives international pH 5-7 and well-drained light loam; rakbankerd gives Thai farmer practice (4.5-6.8, lime adjustment, 30-40 cm tillage); PMC Biology 2022 ties soil salinity to fruit quality.

4_water.rationale: FAO gives 400-600 mm and crop coefficient ranges; NC State adds drip/fertigation guidance; PMC review corroborates water-stress effects on fruit quality.

5_planting.rationale: rakbankerd provides Thai seedling and field-spacing practice; FAO Land & Water gives international spacing density; FAO tomato production manual covers indeterminate/determinate distinction; NC State adds staking and weaving.

6_care.rationale: FAO provides international NPK reference levels (presented as reference, not prescription); NC State covers fertigation; rakbankerd covers Thai practice for blossom-end rot management; explicit deferral to กรมพัฒนาที่ดิน/กรมวิชาการเกษตร for site-specific rates.

7_pests_diseases.rationale: UC IPM provides comprehensive disease/pest taxonomy; KU 'Diseases of Tomato' journal article and KU bacterial wilt monograph anchor Thai relevance; rakbankerd covers Thai biological control practice (Beauveria); WarningBox in place; no specific chemical doses given.

8_harvest.rationale: FAO and rakbankerd both give 70-90 days post-transplant + 90-150 days seed-to-harvest; PMC Biology 2022 supports the mature-green-vs-ripe-red trade-off (vitamin C vs lycopene) for harvest-stage choice.

9_economics.rationale: Market-context discussion is qualitative (no income/profit guarantees per SAFETY_POLICY); KU central-Thailand source establishes commercial viability and FAO smallholder manual frames investment characteristics. Explicit refusal to forecast income reduces the rating to medium.

10_thailand_notes.rationale: All four Thai sources contribute to the regional and use-context breakdown; ภาคเหนือ/กลาง/อีสาน/ใต้ split is grounded in soil and climate realities corroborated across the Thai sources.

11_foreign_knowledge.rationale: Six international sources directly support what foreign authorities say versus what does not transfer to Thailand; explicit Thailand-applicability framing for each.

overall_rationale: Twelve verified sources (six Thai, six international) support every section, with multi-source coverage on every high-rated section and explicit downgrade on the economics section to medium because Kaset Atlas declines income forecasts.
